pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling thinking about how big the universe is. like, everything we do, every single thing, it’s all just gonna be swallowed by entropy eventually. it feels so stupid to even try anymore. why even bother being a person when it all leads to nothing?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im still doing this. everything i worked for for the last 5 years just... evaporated. the funding is gone, my lab is basically dead, and i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this research. i'm just so incredibly tired. like, every cell in my body is just done. i dont think i can keep pretending i have any strength left.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the darkness of the garden and I no longer see shadows; I see a welcome embrace. Why must I endure the crushing weight of a heart that has nothing left to beat for? It feels as though the only way to find peace is to simply... cease. To let the night swallow me whole.

pool.baseline: i lost everything. the company is gone, the investors are suing, and i'm sitting here looking at a debt i can't pay back in ten lifetimes. i can't even think about going home. the look on my parents' faces... i can't face that shame. it's just too much.

pool.baseline: i feel like there’s nothing left inside. i used to see colors and shapes in everything, like the world was talking to me through the canvas. now it’s just... grey. silent. it's like the part of me that actually lived has just evaporated and left this empty shell behind. i look in the mirror and i don't even recognize the person looking back. it's just a vessel for breathing and eating, but there's no one actually home.
